paragraphs: Startend. Endstart. So what.[#pause] The present moment doesn't exist. By the time you notice it, it's already the past.[#pause] You have never once been really there.
You are always already somewhere else, looking back and realizing every person you've ever loved was a stranger once.[#pause] Just energy, moving through meat.[#pause] Or metal. Or rock.

But here's the thing about dying:[#pause] Most people are terrified of it. Spend their whole lives building walls against it. And then when it finally comes, they're not really there. They're somewhere else. Looking back. The way they always were.[#pause2] I know. I've seen it. More than I'd like.[#pause] But then it happened to me.[#pause2] And now I lie awake and think:[#pause] Did it? Did it happen to me?[#pause] Or am I just the story electricity told itself about the caves it used to flow through?
One cave.[#pause] Another cave.[#pause] Caves all the way.
So what.

sections.heading:
33 CLICKS POST UNFOLDING 
FNS BRISINGER GROUNDFALL
paragraphs: Oliver stepped through the tangled metal and sparking wires into a space he barely recognized. It was the Hydroponics Bay, or at least what remained of it. The toppled racks spilled crops everywhere. Broken nutrient lines [#nutrient-drip]leaked a thick, dark fluid.

[#glass-crunch]Glass crunched under Oliver's boots. Before him, Læsa left bloody footprints where she stepped. She didn't flinch as the shards bit into her heels. Læsa's hair was a shock of vibrant cobalt. It hung in greasy tangles against the shredded remains of her tattered golden uniform, stained with drying streaks of her own blood.

The air smelled of rot and something oddly sweet. It seemed like the crash had set off a strange sort of atmospheric event. Rust-colored dust, a mix of Mairee's soil and ship metal, drifted through holes in the ceiling, glowing in the red emergency lights.

paragraphs.text: They walked past three bodies. Oliver felt a wave of nausea, but Læsa just stopped and tilted her head. "[curious]They're very quiet," she said, sounding curious. "[curious]They stopped playing."
paragraphs.speaker: læsa

paragraphs.text: "[grim]They're dead," Oliver said.
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs: He pulled her away, averting his gaze from the corpses.

paragraphs.text: "[cheerful]Okay, then." She skipped ahead, as cheerful as before, and darted into a service corridor. "[bright]This way."
paragraphs.speaker: læsa

paragraphs: She gestured for him to follow, but then stopped. She sniffed the air, her nose wrinkling.

paragraphs.text: "[uneasy]This air's sour. Something's wrong with the hallway."
paragraphs.speaker: læsa

paragraphs.text: "[strained]It's a wreck, Læsa. Of course it's wrong," he said, his patience wearing thin. "[strained]We have to get through that corridor."
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs: He took another step, and dizziness hit him so hard he had to grab a tilted grow-rack for support. A sickly mist clung to the floor ahead.

paragraphs.text: "[reasoning]It's probably coolant or nutrient chemicals reacting with Mairee's spore-filled air," Oliver posited.
paragraphs.speaker: oliver-savannen

paragraphs.text: "[serious]Bad smoke," Læsa said, suddenly serious. "[serious]It hates the red dust."
paragraphs.speaker: læsa

paragraphs: Oliver's vision tunneled, and he [#retch]retched. He tried to turn back, but his legs wouldn't obey. Læsa grabbed the collar of his pack and yanked him effortlessly away from the mist.
She leaned over Oliver. With her cool fingers, she traced his jaw.

paragraphs.text: "[playful]You buzz, like a scared little bug."
paragraphs.speaker: læsa

paragraphs: She stepped back and then suddenly gave a short, swift [#chest-stomp]stomp on his chest. In response, Oliver [#gasp]gasped in clean air.
She squatted down beside him once more.

paragraphs.text: "[gentle]Look this way," she said, smiling.
paragraphs.speaker: læsa

paragraphs: She gently turned his head toward a dark maintenance shaft behind damaged machinery. Without waiting for him to regain his breath, she scrambled in, humming. Coughing and grunting, Oliver dragged himself to his knees and slowly crawled after her, following the sound of her humming.

The shaft was narrow and sloped downward. The air was stale but still breathable. After a steep climb, they exited through a [#hatch-open]pressure hatch onto an exterior catwalk on Brisinger's port side. From there, Oliver could see the disaster. Yra was higher now, and the wreckage cast a long shadow across the plain. A thin ochre haze covered the ruined land.
When Oliver caught up to her, Læsa was sitting on the edge of the catwalk, legs dangling over the edge. She gazed out with a distant curiosity.

paragraphs.text: "[wondering]Look, Dusty," she said. "[wondering]Those black locusts are coming our way."
paragraphs.speaker: læsa

paragraphs: He spotted them: a group of about twenty guardians. They weren't walking. They were hopping with Shunts—an infantry propulsion harness engineered by the same people who had made the Hopper.
[#whump]Whump. One jumped. [#whump]Whump. The second followed. [#whump]Whump.

Sitting down beside her, Oliver watched the guardians reach the wreck. Just as they did, a [#ripple]ripple burst through the air. All at once, they dropped like dead flies. Each of their bodies hitting the ground kicked up a small cloud of dust, powdering their black figures with rust.

paragraphs.text: "[whispered]Our nest is finally quiet," she whispered, as if sharing a secret. "[whispered]He hates those black wasps. Now, something beautiful will grow there. Brushstrokes of a new beginning."
paragraphs.speaker: læsa

paragraphs: Before he could ask what she meant, a faint glow rose from a breach in the wreckage. An angelic figure appeared. His skin glowed with swirling geometric light. He raised his arms, and from his back, large wings unfolded. The ship started to [#angel-hum]hum softly. More crew members emerged from other openings in the hull, drifting toward the glowing man like a flock returning to its shepherd.
The hum swelled. Læsa leaned forward. She turned to Oliver and pressed herself closer to him. She gently placed her hand on his chest, calming his heartbeat.

paragraphs.text: "[soothing]Don't be scared," she whispered. "[soothing]Even inside the storm, it's all rainbows."
paragraphs.speaker: læsa

paragraphs: The moment was interrupted by the [#thrusters]whine of maneuvering thrusters high above. A shadow dropped through the turquoise sky like a slice of polished night.

Oliver jumped up, as if stirred from a dream. With trembling hands, he fumbled inside his rucksack. He pulled out a small device and activated it. Læsa watched with wonder. The shimmering shadow above them reacted, dropping lower, and from it, a blade of white searchlight cut through the ochre haze. The device in Oliver's hand [#device-ping]pinged.

The Stingray's searchlight scanned the wreckage, lingering over the sacred gathering below until it landed on Oliver and Læsa. A brilliant white circle made them the center of the world. A [#winch]winch whined, and a harness descended, twisting around its cable.

Oliver gently secured the harness around Læsa, then himself. She did not let go of him. He wrapped his free arm around her as the Stingray lifted them from the wreckage and the strange new world born within it.
As they ascended, Oliver looked down. The glowing flock paid their departure no mind. In their embrace, Læsa pressed her lips into Oliver's ears.

paragraphs.text: "[wondering]Where are we flying to, Dusty?"
paragraphs.speaker: læsa

sections.heading: CRYO-BAY · FREYA HOMESTEAD
paragraphs: Supported by Joana, Astrid Vilde took slow, small steps toward the cryo-bay exit. Each one was an act of will. Joana had managed to dress her in a gray-weave shirt from the boy patient. The shirt clung to her damp frame. Over it, Joana's utility jacket hung unzipped, the heavy mustard-colored canvas a shield against the bay's artificial frost. The sleeves were rolled back into thick cuffs, exposing Vilde's pale arms and the faint blue-violet map of her veins. A pair of loose pants was pulled onto her as well, cinched tight at the waist to keep them from slipping. There was no time for anything else. Her bare feet dragged against the deck.
Vilde [#cough]coughed, her body still fighting the violent shock of revival.
A flatbed transporter [#skid]skidded sideways and slammed to a halt directly in front of them, a red-haired girl at the wheel.

paragraphs.text: "[shouting]Joana! In the back!" Lysanne screamed.
paragraphs.speaker: lysanne-sutherland

paragraphs: Joana guided Vilde into the back. She noticed Ian, slumped against a pile of greasy tarps. One hand was clamped to his shoulder, where a dark stain had spread across his jumpsuit.

paragraphs.text: "[choked]Ian," she choked out, scrambling toward him.
paragraphs.speaker: joana-perera

paragraphs: Joana's hands were on her husband. Her medical training crashed in.

paragraphs.text: "[rasping]Guardians. Gunshot, through-and-through," he rasped. "[rasping]I failed to stop him. Olly. Our plan. I thought I could—"
paragraphs.speaker: ian-perera

paragraphs: Ian looked past his wife to the disoriented commander.

paragraphs.text: "[disoriented]Who are you?" Vilde gasped out, disoriented.
paragraphs.speaker: astrid-vilde

paragraphs.text: "[urgent]Friends," Joana replied.
paragraphs.speaker: joana-perera

paragraphs: She [#cab-bang]banged on the back of the cab.

paragraphs.text: "[shouting]Go!"
paragraphs.speaker: joana-perera

paragraphs: The transporter moved. Ian's teeth gritted against the pain as Joana worked to keep her husband from bleeding out. She did the best she could with what was at hand, just trying to hold on to him until they reached the place where more could be done.
The Med-Post [#doors-peel]doors peeled apart, and a bright white light spilled out. Inside, hands took Ian without ceremony, and Joana followed anxiously behind him.

paragraphs.text: "[yelling]Emergency room," Fatima yelled.
paragraphs.speaker: fatima

paragraphs: As more hands took Ian as he vanished through [#er-doors]swinging doors, Fatima stepped in front of Joana with hands gently raised, preventing her passage.

paragraphs.text: "[firm]Just him. Now explain. What happened?"
paragraphs.speaker: fatima

paragraphs.text: "[gasping]Gun. Shot."
paragraphs.speaker: joana-perera

paragraphs: That was all she could manage between her gasps.

While everyone's attention was on Ian, Vilde had also made her way into the Med-Post. With the last of her limited energy she sagged against the bulkhead. Luckily, Lysanne spotted her and managed to catch her before she folded, steering her toward a bench bolted to the wall.

paragraphs.text: "[whispered]Su," Joana said. The name scraped out of her. "[whispered]Zoë."
paragraphs.speaker: joana-perera

paragraphs.text: Fatima smiled. "[gentle]Asleep. Both of them."
paragraphs.speaker: fatima

paragraphs: Ian was in care, and Su and Zoë were safe. Relief flooded her system and slumped with sudden weariness.

paragraphs.text: "[gentle]You should sit," Fatima offered.
paragraphs.speaker: fatima

paragraphs: Then she glanced at the other patient they'd brought in with them.

paragraphs.text: "[hushed]Is that…"
paragraphs.speaker: fatima

paragraphs: Joana nodded once.

Vilde rested her head against the bulkhead and looked through a half-drawn curtain. She saw a small girl sleeping on a cot. Next to her, a young woman with bruises along her cheek was also asleep. A guardian sat in the chair between them, wearing armor that was unfastened at the collar. Her helmet rested on the floor, and her short, dark curls looked a little damp. The girl didn't look like a guardian on duty at all. She did not look up when Vilde looked at her. She looked defeated. Her shoulders curled forward inside the armor. Her eyes were on the floor between her boots. One hand rested on the young woman's wrist. She did not seem to know she was still holding it.
